conditions: Generalized anxiety symptoms related to work stress
medications: Adderall XR 15 mg (amphetamine/dextroamphetamine) once daily on workdays
Albuterol HFA rescue inhaler as needed
Fluticasone (Flovent) inhaler twice daily during flare seasons
Sumatriptan 50 mg as needed for migraines
Ondansetron ODT as needed for migraine-associated nausea
health.mental_health: Work-related anxiety and insomnia have increased ahead of a performance review; she’s worried her ADHD symptoms (missed details, time-blindness) are being interpreted as carelessness. She avoids disclosing ADHD at work and is debating whether to request accommodations.
recent_visits: Primary care visit (Feb 2026) to re-check asthma control and refill inhalers
Neurology telehealth follow-up (Jan 2026) for migraine frequency and medication side effects
ADHD medication management appointment (Dec 2025) with a psychiatric nurse practitioner

finance.debt: About $6,800 in credit card debt from a prior car repair, tools, and catching up on rent; she’s also on a payment plan for an ER bill from an asthma flare last year. She’s anxious about losing income if terminated.
recent_transactions: Purchased replacement work boots and insulated gloves (~$210)
Paid late fee on credit card after missing a due date
Co-pay for ADHD medication management visit
Transit + gas expenses increased due to early job-site call times
Sent $150 to her mom to help with utilities after a tight month

relationships: Mother: Tasha Clarke (lives in Dundalk; supportive but worried about job stability)
Younger brother: Malik Clarke (16; Sienna helps with rides and school forms when she can)
Best friend: Jada Robinson (community college classmate; confidante about the performance review)
Coworker mentor: Luis Mendoza (journeyman electrician; gives practical advice but can be blunt)
On-and-off dating: Evan Pierce (bartender in Federal Hill; relationship strained by her schedule and stress)
social.social_media_activity: Mostly Instagram and TikTok; posts occasional photos from the Inner Harbor, thrift finds, and gym selfies. She avoids posting anything about her job sites or employer and recently scrubbed a few venting stories after worrying management might see them.
private_conversations: Texting Jada about fear of termination and whether to disclose ADHD to HR
Discussing with her mom how long she could cover rent if she’s fired
Telling Evan she’s been having panic-y chest tightness that’s not just asthma
Asking Luis if the foreman is building a case against her over mistakes

schedule.work_hours: Typically Mon–Fri 6:30am–3:00pm on commercial sites; occasional Saturdays for overtime or deadlines (7:00am–1:00pm).
regular_appointments: Inhaler refill/peak flow check with primary care twice a year
Weekly evening gym session with Jada (when not on overtime)
upcoming_events: Performance review meeting with foreman and HR (scheduled for next Tuesday at 2:30pm)
Safety re-certification refresher (OSHA 10/CPR) later this month
Apartment lease renewal decision due in 3 weeks
Neurology follow-up for migraines in two weeks
schedule.daily_routine: Up around 5:15am, quick shower, coffee, checks a task list app and packs inhaler and migraine meds. Commutes by car to the site, works in crews, then comes home tired and tries to prep tools and clothes for the next day to avoid forgetting items. Evenings are split between chores, brief workouts, and trying to sleep early, though stress often keeps her scrolling longer than she wants.

professional.company: Mid-size union-affiliated electrical contractor doing commercial tenant fit-outs and light industrial work in the Baltimore metro area
projects: Tenant fit-out wiring for a Harbor East office renovation
Lighting retrofit in a warehouse near Curtis Bay
Troubleshooting intermittent breaker trips at a small medical clinic in Towson
professional.performance_issues: She has been written up twice in the past month for missed punch-list items and a mislabeled panel schedule. She’s also been late to two early call times after a migraine morning and one time-blindness incident. She worries the foreman views her as unreliable and that she’s being compared unfavorably to other apprentices.
workplace_relationships: Foreman (Derek Harlan): strict on punctuality and documentation; has started assigning her less critical tasks
Mentor (Luis Mendoza): helpful but expects her to double-check everything; pushes her to use checklists
HR contact (Monique Harris): only met during onboarding; Sienna is considering reaching out about accommodations but fears stigma
Crewmate (Alyssa Nguyen): friendly and shares notes on code requirements; also competing for steadier hours

hobbies: DIY home fixes and small electronics tinkering
Sketching and digital art on her phone/tablet
Exploring coffee shops around Hampden and Station North
habits: Uses a medication reminder app but still occasionally forgets doses during chaotic mornings
Keeps an inhaler in her tool bag and one at home
Drinks 2–3 coffees/energy drinks on early shifts, which can worsen migraines
Stress-spending on small purchases (food delivery, thrift items) when anxious about work
Vapes nicotine socially when stressed, especially after difficult days (she hides this from her mom)
lifestyle.living_situation: Rents a small two-bedroom rowhouse apartment in West Baltimore with a roommate; she’s behind on one utility bill and is worried she’ll need to move back in with her mom if she loses her job.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: No strict diet; tries to eat high-protein meals for energy at work but often ends up with convenience food on job sites. Avoids known migraine triggers like red wine and sometimes limits chocolate; keeps salty snacks around because she skips lunch when busy.
